title: perf(art): back art-job stores with SQLite (drop full-file parse+rewrite)
intent: Finishes item 5 of PERFORMANCE_AUDIT_2026-07-04.md. The two file-based art-job stores parsed and rewrote the entire ~730KB data/art_jobs.json on every create/read (measured 22ms/parse, unbounded append-only growth) and accidentally shared one file with two incompatible record shapes. Each store now uses its own dedicated SQLite table with a one-time shape-filtered migration; public signatures are unchanged so no router call site is touched.

diff_articulation.what_changed: art_job_store.py (singular: id/lane/payload) now reads/writes a dedicated art_studio_jobs SQLite table via get_rmos_db(), with a one-time transaction-wrapped migration that imports only lane/payload-shaped rows from the legacy JSON; dirty rows are logged, duplicate legacy ids preserve first-read legacy behavior instead of aborting, invalid payload JSON is retained under a raw-payload wrapper, and create uses a single SQL ON CONFLICT upsert instead of delete-then-insert. art_jobs_store.py (plural: id/job_type/rings/...) still delegates runtime reads to SQLiteArtJobsStore (art_jobs table), but migration now imports job_type-shaped legacy rows transactionally, preserves legacy created_at as an ISO instant when possible, skips duplicate ids without aborting, and uses single-statement upsert for runtime re-registration. Both stores bind init/migration state to the active RMOS DB identity under a lock, so tests/workers that reset DB state do not silently reuse stale globals. Public signatures remain unchanged for rosette_jobs_routes, root_art_router, rosette_jobs_router, rmos rosette_cam_router, and pipeline_ops_rosette. The legacy data/art_jobs.json is retained as the migration source.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: Removes a measured per-request full-file parse+rewrite on live rosette job create/read paths and untangles two record shapes that shared one file (the plural typed reader crashed on singular rows and vice versa). Not cosmetic: it changes the persistence backend while preserving the public contract, with a shape-filtered one-time data migration and new tests.

commands_run: pytest tests/test_art_job_stores_migration.py -q -> 11 passed (round-trip, upsert-no-dup, shape-filtered migration, dirty legacy rows, duplicate legacy ids, deterministic ordering, DB reset rebinding, interrupted migration retry, migrate-once)

attempted tests/test_art_studio_api_endpoint_smoke.py as part of the original route bundle -> local optional art-studio routers unavailable because defusedxml is not installed; non-optional affected route slices above passed

files.intent: Singular art-job store: SQLite-backed art_studio_jobs table + transaction-wrapped one-time JSON migration; single-statement upsert create.
files.behavior_change: Reads/writes SQLite instead of rewriting the whole JSON file; one-time migration of lane/payload rows with logging for skipped/dirty rows; duplicate legacy ids preserve first row; create is now a single SQL upsert. Public signatures unchanged.

files.intent: Plural art-job store: delegate to SQLiteArtJobsStore + transaction-wrapped one-time JSON migration of job_type-shaped rows.
files.behavior_change: Reads/writes SQLite via SQLiteArtJobsStore; legacy created_at is preserved as an ISO string where possible; duplicate legacy ids preserve first row; runtime create uses single SQL upsert. Public signatures unchanged.

files.intent: Cover round-trip, upsert, shape-filtered migration, dirty legacy rows, duplicate legacy ids, deterministic ordering, DB reset rebinding, interrupted migration retry, and migrate-once.

architecture_scan.acknowledgement_notes: Storage-backend change with preserved public contract and a one-time, shape-filtered, idempotent data migration; legacy JSON retained as source. Follow-up hardening replaces delete-then-insert with SQL upsert, logs skipped/dirty rows, preserves duplicate-id first-read migration behavior, binds module globals to the active RMOS DB identity, and proves interrupted singular migration retries cleanly. Verified by existing route/store slices plus 11 focused migration tests.
